title: Role Mapping
lede: Role Mapping links each Contact Center role to an Access role and a View profile, so members automatically get the right permissions when their role is set. This tab is available to User-Management users.

steps.title: Open the Role Mapping tab
steps.content:
1. Go to Call Center → Contact Center → Groups and select a group.
  2. Open the Role Mapping tab. It appears only for the main account or a sub-account with user management enabled.

images.caption: Role Mapping: allowed lists plus the role-to-access mapping.
Role Mapping: allowed lists plus the role-to-access mapping.

steps.title: Choose the allowed access roles and view profiles
steps.content: In the Allowed access roles & view profiles card, pick which Access roles and which View profiles may be used in the mapping below. These lists bound the dropdowns in the next step — they don't limit what you can assign elsewhere in User Management.

steps.title: Map each Contact Center role
steps.content:
In the Contact Center role → Access role & View profile card, for each role — Agent, Monitor / QA, Supervisor, Admin — pick the Access role and View profile a member with that role should get.

Leave a cell empty to leave that value unchanged for members of that role.

steps.title: Save to apply the mapping
steps.content: Click Save. The mapping is applied to every member based on their current Contact Center role (set on the Members Roles tab). The main account is never remapped. Empty mappings never clear a member's existing values.

nextSteps.title: What's Next?
nextSteps.description: Set member roles and route calls to the group.

cards.title: Members & Roles
cards.description: Set the Contact Center role that drives the mapping.
